what_it_does: Creates a static 1600x900 presentation-style HTML view showing a transition from fragmented current practice to a unified "One Space" approach, using two large comparison cards, a plug-style connector, blueprint underlays, a generated clutter panel, and a calm hub SVG panel.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and design tokens from /surface/styles/vars.css.
The main composition is a centered fixed-size .view with absolute-positioned title, cards, connector, mark, and footer band.
Left card represents "Current Practice" with a JS-populated clutter field and bullets about fragmented information, many accounts/files/channels, and slow error-prone work.
Right card represents "Our Approach — "One Space"" with a gold-line hub diagram and bullets about centralised access, clearer communication, and saving time and cost.
The connector visually combines a bronze socket, dashed line, gold prongs, and plug body between the two cards.
A v2.1 correction overrides the footer band into a thin quiet foot, hides its contents, and lifts the V mark.
standouts: Two large zones carry the main before-and-after structure.
Fragmented-versus-unified contrast is expressed through both copy and media treatment.
The plug connector makes the transition between cards explicit.
The calm solution graphic uses gold stroke outlines rather than gold filled areas.
conventions: Single-file HTML with inline CSS and a small inline script.
Relies heavily on CSS custom properties from the shared surface variables file.
Uses short semantic class names for visual parts such as .cards, .card, .media, .clutter, .calm, .connect, and .gband.
Comments document layout zones, intent, and later correction overrides.
Breaks responsive web convention by fixing the canvas at 1600px by 900px.
Uses later !important overrides instead of removing or rewriting the earlier footer styles.
concerns: The fixed 1600x900 canvas is not responsive within this file.
The absolute /surface/styles/vars.css dependency must exist at runtime for tokens to resolve.
The footer band is defined fully and then substantially overridden with !important rules, leaving hidden markup and inactive earlier styling in place.
The clutter tile script assumes the #clutter element exists and uses hard-coded tile geometry.
The file depends on color-mix() support for the clutter background.
notes: A static comparison slide that frames scattered operational inputs as moving into a unified one-space solution.
questions: Should the hidden footer label remain in the DOM after the v2.1 correction?
Is the 1600x900 fixed canvas intended for slide export rather than browser-responsive viewing?
Are the clutter tile coordinates tuned to a specific media box size?
Should the external Google Font dependency be acceptable for the target runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents a current-to-solution transition with large zones and an explicit movement connector.
apparent_themes: warm neutral design with gold emphasis
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make a scattered current workflow feel visibly fragmented while presenting the proposed "One Space" approach as ordered, centralized, and calmer.
novelty: The plug-and-socket connector between the two comparison cards is a distinct visual device within the file.
contribution: Contributes a two-zone current-to-solution comparison pattern with generated clutter, a calm hub diagram, and a physicalized movement connector that a fused system could keep as a transition-oriented visual substrate.
summary_for_embedding: Static 1600x900 HTML slide comparing fragmented current practice with a unified "One Space" approach through two large cards, generated clutter tiles, a gold hub SVG, and a plug connector.
intention_for_embedding: Built to communicate movement from many scattered accounts, files, and channels into one centralized operational space.
